title: Crunchy Korean Herring Sujebi
description: A peppery and bouncy Korean dish featuring herring, this lacto-vegetarian-friendly sujebi is air-fried to perfection, offering an adventurous twist on traditional Korean cuisine. It combines the flavors of the sea with a satisfying crunch.

ingredients.name: herring fillets
ingredients.notes: drained and cut into small pieces

instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together flour, cornstarch, salt, black pepper, garlic powder, and paprika.
Add the herring pieces to the bowl and toss to coat evenly with the flour mixture.
In a separate bowl, whisk together eggs and milk.
Add the egg mixture to the herring mixture and stir until just combined.
Heat the vegetable oil in an air fryer to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Using a spoon, drop small balls of the herring mixture into the air fryer basket, leaving about 1 inch of space between each sujebi.
Air-fry the sujebi for 5 minutes, shaking the basket halfway through.
Increase the air fryer temperature to 420 degrees Fahrenheit and continue to air-fry for an additional 2 minutes, or until the sujebi are golden brown and crispy.
Remove the sujebi from the air fryer and serve hot.
Garnish with chopped green onions and a sprinkle of toasted sesame seeds, if desired.
